homepage.heroSubline: Local electrical solutions built for North Texas homes. Serving Dallas neighborhoods since 2012 with reliable, code-compliant work.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Onyx Electrical began as a small team servicing historic homes in Oak Lawn. Today, we've grown to serve the entire DFW metroplex while maintaining that neighborhood electrician approach.
We believe electrical work should be predictable - clear pricing, thorough explanations, and solutions tailored to your home's age and wiring. No upsells, just honest assessments.
When hail storms damage panels in Lake Highlands or older homes in Bishop Arts need rewiring, we're proud to be the crew locals call first.

valueProps.title: DFW-Tested Expertise
valueProps.description: We know how Texas heat impacts wiring and which solutions work best for 1950s ranches versus modern Frisco builds.

valueProps.title: Storm-Ready Response
valueProps.description: When spring thunderstorms knock out power in Plano, our team prioritizes emergency repairs for families and seniors.

valueProps.title: Historic Home Specialists
valueProps.description: From knob-and-tube in McKinney's downtown to aluminum wiring in Arlington ranches, we handle delicate updates safely.

valueProps.title: No Surprise Pricing
valueProps.description: Upfront quotes with line-item breakdowns so you understand exactly what your Dallas home needs.

homepage.ctaSubline: Same-day estimates available for Dallas, Fort Worth, and surrounding areas.

services.name: Electrical Safety Inspections
services.shortDescription: Comprehensive evaluations to identify potential hazards in your DFW home.
services.description:
North Texas weather takes a toll on electrical systems. Our 75-point inspection checks for storm damage in Plano subdivisions, outdated wiring in McKinney historic districts, and overloaded circuits in Frisco's newer smart homes.

We specifically look for Dallas-area concerns like hail-damaged outdoor panels, humidity-corroded connections in Arlington's 1960s ranches, and insufficient grounding for lightning protection. Reports include photos and prioritized recommendations.

Many Dallas homeowners schedule annual pre-storm season checks, especially in hail-prone areas like Lake Highlands. We'll show you exactly what needs immediate attention versus future upgrades.
process: Step 1: Review home's age and previous electrical work
Step 2: Test all outlets, switches, and major appliances
Step 3: Inspect panel, wiring, and grounding systems
Step 4: Deliver digital report with repair priorities
benefits: Prevent electrical fires in older Dallas homes
Identify storm damage before it causes outages
Peace of mind for growing families

faq.question: How often should my Fort Worth home be inspected?
faq.answer: We recommend annual checks for pre-1980 homes and every 2-3 years for newer constructions, with additional visits after major storms.

faq.question: Do you check for aluminum wiring risks?
faq.answer: Yes, our Dallas inspections specifically evaluate aluminum wiring connections common in 1960s-70s Arlington homes for oxidation and overheating signs.

services.name: Electrical Panel Upgrades
services.shortDescription: Modernizing outdated panels to handle today's power demands safely.
services.description:
Many Dallas-area homes still operate with 60-amp panels that can't support modern appliances and electronics. We specialize in upgrading these systems without disrupting your daily life.

For Preston Hollow's historic estates, we carefully preserve original features while installing 200-amp panels with whole-home surge protection. In Frisco's newer developments, we integrate smart panels that let homeowners monitor usage via app.

Texas heat means air conditioners draw heavy loads - we size panels appropriately so your system won't trip breakers during August heatwaves. All upgrades include city permitting and final inspection coordination.
process: Step 1: Assess current panel and household power needs
Step 2: Obtain necessary Dallas city permits
Step 3: Install new panel with minimal downtime
Step 4: Test all circuits and complete city inspection
benefits: Support home additions and EV chargers
Reduce fire risks in older panels

faq.question: How long does a panel upgrade take in Plano?
faq.answer: Most single-family home upgrades take 6-8 hours. We schedule them weekdays to minimize disruption.

faq.question: Will my power be off during the upgrade?
faq.answer: We coordinate temporary power solutions so you'll only be without electricity for about 2 hours during the actual panel swap.

services.name: Interior & Exterior Lighting
services.shortDescription: Beautiful, functional lighting solutions for Dallas homes.
services.description:
From preserving the character of Bishop Arts bungalows to illuminating Uptown high-rises, we design lighting that enhances your space. Our team handles everything from basic fixture swaps to whole-home LED conversions.

We pay special attention to Dallas' climate - installing humidity-resistant outdoor fixtures in Oak Lawn and motion-activated security lighting for Preston Hollow estates. For historic districts like McKinney's downtown, we source period-appropriate reproductions that meet modern safety standards.

Smart lighting installations include thorough homeowner training so you can control systems effortlessly. We'll help you choose energy-efficient options that reduce cooling costs during Texas summers.
process: Step 1: Consultation to understand lighting goals and home style
Step 2: Present fixture options with energy estimates
Step 3: Professional installation with attention to detail
Step 4: Final walkthrough to ensure perfect operation
benefits: Reduce energy bills with LED conversions

faq.question: Can you match my historic home's existing fixtures?
faq.answer: Yes, we maintain relationships with specialty suppliers to find authentic matches for Dallas' older properties.

faq.question: What's the best outdoor lighting for Fort Worth's climate?
faq.answer: We recommend powder-coated aluminum fixtures with gasketed seals to withstand humidity and temperature swings.

services.name: Standby Generator Installation
services.shortDescription: Keep your Dallas home powered during Texas storms.
services.description:
When severe weather knocks out power across DFW, our automatic standby generators keep homes safe and comfortable. We specialize in whole-house systems sized for Texas-sized needs.

For Frisco's larger new constructions, we install 22kW systems that run AC units during outages. In Arlington's established neighborhoods, we design compact solutions that fit smaller lots while powering essentials.

Every installation includes thorough homeowner training and annual maintenance checks before storm season. We handle all permitting and work closely with local gas companies for proper hookups.
process: Step 1: Home energy audit to determine power needs
Step 2: Select appropriately sized generator
Step 3: Professional installation with automatic transfer switch
Step 4: Comprehensive operation tutorial
benefits: Peace of mind for elderly residents

faq.question: How loud are standby generators in Plano subdivisions?
faq.answer: Modern units operate at about 60 decibels - quieter than a normal conversation. We position them to minimize neighborhood impact.

faq.question: What maintenance do Dallas generators require?
faq.answer: We recommend biannual checkups - before and after storm season - to ensure reliable operation when you need it most.

services.name: EV Charger Installation
services.shortDescription: Future-proof your DFW home with dedicated electric vehicle charging.
services.description:
As more Dallas residents switch to electric vehicles, we're helping homeowners add convenient charging solutions. Our installations range from basic 240V outlets to full charging stations with smart capabilities.

We evaluate your panel capacity first - many Plano homes from the 1980s need upgrades to support EV charging. For Frisco's newer builds, we often install hardwired 48-amp units with WiFi monitoring.

All work includes proper permitting and meets Dallas city code requirements. We'll help you position the charger for easiest access while maintaining your home's curb appeal.
process: Step 1: Assess electrical panel capacity and garage layout
Step 2: Recommend appropriate charger type
Step 3: Install dedicated circuit with proper safety disconnects
Step 4: Test system and demonstrate operation
benefits: Charge overnight while rates are low

faq.question: Can my Arlington ranch's electrical panel handle an EV charger?
faq.answer: We'll test your system first - many 1960s homes need panel upgrades, which we can complete before charger installation.

faq.question: What's the typical installation cost in McKinney?
faq.answer: Basic 240V outlet installs start around $600, while full charging stations range $1,200-$2,500 depending on your home's existing infrastructure.

services.name: Ceiling Fan Installation
services.shortDescription: Improve comfort and energy efficiency in your DFW home.
services.description:
Proper ceiling fans can reduce Dallas cooling costs by up to 40% during our long summers. We install and repair fans in all types of local homes - from Deep Ellum lofts to Lake Highlands ranches.

Our team pays special attention to older homes, ensuring new fans mount securely to ceiling joists without damaging original plaster. For vaulted ceilings in Plano's newer builds, we install extended downrods for optimal air circulation.

We'll help you select the right size and style for each room, considering factors like Texas humidity and room dimensions. All installations include thorough balancing to prevent wobbling noises.
process: Step 1: Evaluate room size and ceiling structure
Step 2: Help select appropriate fan size/style
Step 3: Professional installation with proper support
Step 4: Balance and test all functions
benefits: Reduce AC reliance during Texas summers

faq.question: Can you install fans in my historic McKinney home's plaster ceilings?
faq.answer: Yes, we use specialized mounting systems that distribute weight safely without compromising historic integrity.

faq.question: What size fan works best for Fort Worth bedrooms?
faq.answer: Most master bedrooms need 52-56" fans, while smaller rooms do well with 44" models. We'll measure your space to recommend the perfect fit.

services.name: Outdoor Lighting Solutions
services.shortDescription: Enhance safety and curb appeal with professional outdoor lighting.
services.description:
Dallas homeowners rely on outdoor lighting for both security and entertainment spaces. We design systems that withstand Texas weather while complementing your home's architecture.

For Uptown townhomes, we install sleek path lighting that guides guests safely. Preston Hollow estates often choose moonlit tree uplighting that showcases mature oaks. All our fixtures use durable materials that resist corrosion from North Texas humidity.

Smart systems let you control lighting from your phone - perfect for turning lights on remotely when traveling. We also install motion-activated security lighting that deters intruders without disturbing neighbors.
process: Step 1: Walk property to identify lighting opportunities
Step 2: Design system balancing function and aesthetics
Step 3: Install weatherproof fixtures with proper wiring
Step 4: Program controls and demonstrate operation

faq.question: What's the best lighting for my Frisco home's pool area?
faq.answer: We recommend wet-rated LED fixtures around pools - they're energy efficient and won't degrade from constant splashing.

faq.question: Can outdoor lighting help with Dallas home security?
faq.answer: Absolutely. Well-placed motion lights and consistently illuminated entryways are proven deterrents against property crime.

reviews.name: Martha K.
reviews.text: Our 1962 Arlington ranch's original fuse box was a fire hazard. Onyx's team explained every step of the upgrade process and completed the work in one day with minimal disruption. The new 200-amp panel handles our central AC and new kitchen appliances perfectly.

reviews.name: Derrick T.
reviews.text: After a hailstorm damaged our exterior electrical in Plano, Onyx had a crew out same-day. They replaced our weatherhead and meter base quickly, then helped file the insurance claim with detailed documentation. Five stars for emergency response!

reviews.name: Lisa W.
reviews.text: The electrician spent two hours assessing our McKinney historic district home's wiring before recommending the perfect charger location. Installation was flawless, and they even programmed our ChargePoint app for us. Couldn't be happier.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: Buying a 1940s Dallas bungalow with knob-and-tube wiring scared us until we met Onyx. Their crew preserved original plaster while completely updating our electrical system. The project manager kept us informed daily, and everything passed inspection first try.

reviews.name: Nina P.
reviews.text: Our Frisco home's great room needed proper airflow. Onyx installed three large fans on vaulted ceilings with perfect balance - no wobbling at all. They even matched our existing finishes exactly. Highly recommend for anyone in DFW.

reviews.name: James L.
reviews.text: When the February ice storm left our Preston Hollow neighborhood without power for days, we decided never again. Onyx's 22kW Generac installation was smooth, and their maintenance plan gives us confidence it'll work when needed.

service-areas.title: Onyx Electrical Service Areas | DFW Electricians
service-areas.description: Serving Dallas, Fort Worth, Plano, Frisco, McKinney, Arlington and surrounding North Texas communities.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Electrical provides reliable electrical services for Fort Worth's charming Craftsman bungalows and historic neighborhoods. Our team is experienced in upgrading outdated wiring, installing ceiling fans, and ensuring your home's electrical system can handle modern demands. With hot, humid summers, we also specialize in surge protection and HVAC electrical support to keep your home comfortable year-round. Whether you're near downtown or in the cultural district, we deliver prompt, professional service tailored to Fort Worth's unique architectural styles.
serviceAreas.localContext: Fort Worth blends historic charm with modern amenities, featuring Craftsman bungalows and a vibrant arts scene.

serviceAreas.content: Serving Plano's established subdivisions, Onyx Electrical offers expert solutions for 1980s-90s homes needing electrical updates. From panel upgrades to outdoor lighting installations, we ensure your home meets current safety standards. Plano's hot summers make whole-house surge protection and energy-efficient lighting a smart investment. Our team understands the needs of older homes in areas like Legacy West and Willow Bend, providing personalized service to keep your electrical systems running smoothly.
serviceAreas.localContext: Plano is known for its well-maintained suburban neighborhoods and thriving corporate hub.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Electrical is Frisco's trusted electrician for newer construction homes built in the 2000s-2020s. We specialize in smart home integrations, EV charger installations, and code-compliant electrical inspections for fast-growing neighborhoods. Frisco's rapid development demands experienced electricians who understand modern building standards. Whether you're in Starwood or Phillips Creek Ranch, our team delivers cutting-edge electrical solutions tailored to North Texas' hot climate, including whole-home backup generator installations.
serviceAreas.localContext: Frisco features master-planned communities with state-of-the-art homes and commercial developments.

serviceAreas.content: For McKinney's historic downtown homes and growing suburbs, Onyx Electrical provides period-appropriate electrical upgrades that preserve charm while improving safety. We expertly rewire antique properties and install discreet modern conveniences. McKinney's hot summers require robust cooling systems—we ensure your electrical panel can handle increased HVAC loads. From Chestnut Square to Stonebridge Ranch, our services include knob-and-tube replacement and landscape lighting that complements historic architecture.
serviceAreas.localContext: McKinney combines a picturesque historic district with rapid suburban expansion.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Electrical serves Arlington's 1960s-70s ranch homes with complete electrical retrofits and panel upgrades. Many homes in this area still have outdated aluminum wiring—we specialize in safe copper replacements. Arlington's extreme summer heat makes proper circuit loading for air conditioning systems essential. Whether you're near the entertainment district or in older neighborhoods like Interlochen, we provide dependable service including whole-house rewiring and GFCI installations for pool areas.
serviceAreas.localContext: Arlington features mid-century homes mixed with major sports and entertainment venues.

serviceAreas.content: In Allen, Onyx Electrical delivers premium electrical services for upscale suburban homes and commercial properties. We specialize in high-end lighting installations, home theater wiring, and whole-home automation systems. Allen's growth demands electricians who understand both new construction standards and the needs of established neighborhoods like Twin Creeks. Our team ensures your electrical infrastructure can handle Texas summers, with services including subpanel installations for backyard kitchens and pool areas.
serviceAreas.localContext: Allen boasts top-rated schools and carefully planned residential communities.

serviceAreas.content: Onyx Electrical serves Richardson's mix of mid-century modern homes and tech-forward businesses. We excel in updating 1960s-80s electrical systems to handle today's digital lifestyles. Richardson's proximity to the Telecom Corridor means we frequently install dedicated circuits for home offices and network equipment. From the Heights neighborhood to Canyon Creek, we provide solutions like USB outlet installations and whole-home surge protection to safeguard electronics during North Texas' intense thunderstorms.
serviceAreas.localContext: Richardson combines established neighborhoods with a high concentration of technology companies.

serviceAreas.content: For Grapevine's historic homes and luxury properties near the lake, Onyx Electrical provides custom electrical solutions that blend seamlessly with architectural character. We specialize in wine cellar wiring, outdoor living space electrification, and chandelier installations for high ceilings. Grapevine's extreme summer temperatures require robust electrical systems for pool equipment and outdoor cooling solutions. Whether you're in the historic district or newer developments like Silver Lake, our team delivers artisanal electrical work with precision.
serviceAreas.localContext: Grapevine offers charming historic districts alongside upscale lakeside living.
